provenance.motif_description: Time-bound requirement specifying when a regulated entity must complete an action or submit information
provenance.rationale: Temporal boundaries are domain-universal: business registration deadlines, tax payment deadlines, filing deadlines. Core regulatory control mechanism.
provenance.promotion_policy: Motifs promoted if: 1) appear in 2+ domains, 2) >100 total supporting rules, 3) >20% minority domain representation, 4) genuine semantic overlap (not predicate coincidence). Deferred motifs lack sufficient evidence or balance.

provenance.motif_description: Required documentation package or set of information elements that must be submitted or maintained for regulatory compliance
provenance.rationale: All three domains require structured document submission: enterprise investor documents, tax filing packages, labor permit and report dossiers. Narrowed from "submission requirement" to emphasize documentation specificity.
provenance.support_evidence: 67 enterprise rules + 154 tax rules + 55 labor rules = 276 total
provenance.promotion_policy: Motifs promoted if: 1) appear in 2+ domains, 2) >100 total supporting rules, 3) >20% minority domain representation, 4) genuine semantic overlap (not predicate coincidence). Deferred motifs lack sufficient evidence or balance.

provenance.motif_description: Conditional exclusion or exemption from a base regulatory requirement under specified circumstances
provenance.rationale: Universal pattern: rules have standard requirements plus exceptions. Enterprise: charter-based exceptions. Tax: status-based exceptions. Domain-agnostic logical structure.
provenance.promotion_policy: Motifs promoted if: 1) appear in 2+ domains, 2) >100 total supporting rules, 3) >20% minority domain representation, 4) genuine semantic overlap (not predicate coincidence). Deferred motifs lack sufficient evidence or balance.

provenance.motif_description: Quantitative boundary or numerical limit that triggers regulatory obligations or changes applicability of rules
provenance.rationale: Numerical boundaries define rule scope across domains. Enterprise: capital thresholds. Tax: revenue thresholds. Quantitative logic is universal.
provenance.promotion_policy: Motifs promoted if: 1) appear in 2+ domains, 2) >100 total supporting rules, 3) >20% minority domain representation, 4) genuine semantic overlap (not predicate coincidence). Deferred motifs lack sufficient evidence or balance.

provenance.motif_description: Condition or criterion determining when a regulatory requirement applies to a specific entity or transaction
provenance.rationale: Narrowed definition: focuses on scoping (when does a rule apply?) vs broad applicability. More precise than previous term.
provenance.promotion_policy: Motifs promoted if: 1) appear in 2+ domains, 2) >100 total supporting rules, 3) >20% minority domain representation, 4) genuine semantic overlap (not predicate coincidence). Deferred motifs lack sufficient evidence or balance.

provenance.motif_description: Consequence or status change that results from compliance or non-compliance with a regulatory requirement
provenance.rationale: All three domains specify consequences: enterprise legal status changes, tax liability and penalties, labor permit revocation or penalty outcomes. Legal effects are fundamental to regulatory enforcement.
provenance.support_evidence: 97 enterprise rules + 39 tax rules + 19 labor rules = 155 total
provenance.promotion_policy: Motifs promoted if: 1) appear in 2+ domains, 2) >100 total supporting rules, 3) >20% minority domain representation, 4) genuine semantic overlap (not predicate coincidence). Deferred motifs lack sufficient evidence or balance.
